headlines.title: Israeli opposition signal foreign policy change in style, but not substance
headlines.snippet: Israeli opposition figures are signaling a potential shift in the style of foreign policy, though not its fundamental objectives. They criticize current methods but do not oppose the core goals.

headlines.title: Seven more sentenced over Texas ICE detention centre shooting
headlines.snippet: US prosecutors allege that individuals involved in the Prairieland Detention Center protest in Texas were connected to antifa. Seven additional individuals have been sentenced in connection with the shooting at the ICE detention center.

headlines.title: Israel kills three Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone strike near a hospital and fuel station in Gaza killed at least three people, according to the Wafa news agency. This incident occurred despite a reported ceasefire.

headlines.title: As Venezuela responds to earthquake devastation, volunteers take charge
headlines.snippet: As frustration grows over the government's disaster response in Venezuela, ordinary citizens have taken the initiative to organize and distribute aid. This is happening amidst the devastation caused by earthquakes.

headlines.title: One week on from Venezuela’s deadly earthquakes
headlines.snippet: More than 1,900 people have been confirmed dead one week after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela. Tens of thousands remain missing in the aftermath of the disaster.

headlines.title: Iranian deputy foreign minister says channel to be set up for MoU breaches
headlines.snippet: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi announced that a communication channel will be established to address breaches of the memorandum of understanding between the US and Iran. Following meetings with Qatari mediators, he also stated that a portion of frozen Iranian assets would be used for essential goods.

headlines.title: Spanish government ‘quietly bans’ use of Palantir over national security concerns
headlines.snippet: The Spanish government has reportedly moved to ban the US surveillance firm Palantir due to national security concerns. This unofficial ban is driven by fears of potential leaks of national security information and threats to national sovereignty.

headlines.title: Gaza interim administration says talks in Cyprus ‘highly productive’
headlines.snippet: The National Committee for the Administration of Gaza (Ncag) concluded two days of productive talks in Cyprus. Discussions with experts and advisors focused on efforts to alleviate the suffering of the Palestinian people.

headlines.title: Israeli Knesset passes bill limiting mosque loudspeakers
headlines.snippet: The Israeli Knesset has passed the preliminary reading of a bill that would limit the use of loudspeakers in mosques. The bill, initiated by a far-right party, would require permits for such systems and grant police authority to enforce the rules.

headlines.title: Lebanon’s president defends Lebanon-Israel framework agreement
headlines.snippet: Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun has defended the country's ceasefire framework agreement with Israel. He stated that Lebanon negotiated on its own behalf and has not relinquished its principles in the agreement, despite protests.

headlines.title: Russian strikes in Ukraine kill six, wound dozens
headlines.snippet: Russian strikes in Ukraine resulted in six deaths and wounded dozens of people, according to Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Lebanon, Syria join CENTCOM-led Middle East security dialogue for first time
headlines.snippet: Lebanon and Syria have joined a Middle East security dialogue led by CENTCOM for the first time, as reported by Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Giant Container Ship ‘Notre-Dame’ Arrives in France
headlines.snippet: The giant container ship ‘Notre-Dame’ has arrived in France, according to Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Israeli Troops to Stay in Lebanon, Syria, Gaza: Katz
headlines.snippet: Israeli troops are reportedly preparing to remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza, as stated by Katz, according to Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: US Signs Agreement to Build Permanent Embassy in Jerusalem
headlines.snippet: The United States has signed an agreement to construct a permanent embassy in Jerusalem, as reported by Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Salah fitness race in focus for Egypt ahead of Australia clash
headlines.snippet: Mohamed Salah is in a race against time to recover from a strain and be fit for Egypt's match against Australia. He is continuing his recovery process.

headlines.title: Iran talks in Qatar on implementing MoU with US concluded
headlines.snippet: Iran's deputy foreign minister confirmed that talks in the Qatari capital concerning the implementation of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding have concluded.

headlines.title: Mixed reaction as Syria's remaining 70 MPs announced
headlines.snippet: Syria's parliament is set to open on July 6th with the announcement of its final 70 Members of Parliament.

headlines.title: Morocco: Rights group says Gen Z activist's sentence 'worrying'
headlines.snippet: Morocco's AMDH rights group has described the sentencing of Gen Z activist Zineb Kharroubi as a "worrying political choice that further undermines civil liberties."

headlines.title: Peace Council to launch pilot shelter project in Gaza: report
headlines.snippet: Reports from Israeli media suggest that humanitarian shelters may be established in parts of Gaza. Additionally, a multinational security force could potentially be deployed to the Strip within weeks.

headlines.title: مونديال الحسم... فرنسا والنرويج تتألقان وترقب للمواجهات القادمة
headlines.snippet: The 'Decisive World Cup' saw strong performances from France and Norway, with anticipation building for upcoming matches, according to almayadeen.net.

headlines.title: عراقتشي: أي تهديد يستهدف الشعب الإيراني أو قيادته سيقابَل بردٍّ فوري وحازم
headlines.snippet: Araghchi stated that any threat against the Iranian people or leadership would be met with an immediate and firm response, as reported by almayadeen.net.

headlines.title: المنتخب النرويجي يتخطى عقبة ساحل العاج ويتأهل لدور الـ16 في مونديال 2026
headlines.snippet: The Norwegian national team has advanced to the Round of 16 in the 2026 World Cup after defeating Ivory Coast, according to almayadeen.net.

headlines.title: مستشفيات جنوبي لبنان تواصل لملمة جراحها بعد العدوان الإسرائيلي
headlines.snippet: Hospitals in southern Lebanon are continuing to recover from the aftermath of Israeli attacks, as reported by almayadeen.net.

headlines.title: مسؤولون إسرائيليون: الجيش الإسرائيلي يستعد لبقاء "طويل" في جنوب لبنان
headlines.snippet: Israeli officials are indicating that the Israeli army is preparing for an extended presence in southern Lebanon, according to almayadeen.net.

headlines.title: Gharibabadi: Doha talks focused on US violations of MoU by US, frozen asstes
headlines.snippet: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs stated that talks in Doha focused on US violations of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, particularly concerning the ceasefire in Lebanon. A working group was formed to monitor implementation.

headlines.title: Armed forces on high alert as Iran prepares for Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral
headlines.snippet: Iran's armed forces have increased their presence along the country's borders to ensure security during funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei. This announcement was made by an army spokesperson.

headlines.title: Millions to attend martyr Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral procession, foundation says
headlines.snippet: The Martyrs and Veterans Affairs Foundation announced that the funeral procession for Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei will span multiple days across Iran. Millions are expected to attend this significant national event.

headlines.title: Iran says missile and drone capabilities are non-negotiable
headlines.snippet: Iran's acting defense minister declared that the country's missile and drone capabilities are a non-negotiable "red line" for national security.

headlines.title: Iran warns Israel: Any threat against Leader, people will meet firm response
headlines.snippet: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi issued a warning that any threat against Iran's people and leadership would be met with an immediate and powerful response. This statement was in reaction to remarks from Israel's war minister.

headlines.title: Talks btw Iran, US for a final agreement not yet started
headlines.snippet: Talks between Iran and the US aimed at reaching a final agreement have not yet commenced, according to Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Any threat against Iranians to receive immediate response
headlines.snippet: Any threat directed at Iranians will be met with an immediate response, as reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Iran’s missile, UAV capabilities nonnegotiable: Defense min.
headlines.snippet: Iran’s missile and UAV capabilities are non-negotiable, according to the defense minister, as reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Israeli forces blow up homes in three south Lebanon villages
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have destroyed homes in three villages in southern Lebanon, as reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Iran presents proposals for new SCO bank
headlines.snippet: Iran has presented proposals for the establishment of a new SCO bank, according to Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Iranians barely visible in US-Iran deal, UN rapporteur says - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: A UN rapporteur stated that Iranians have minimal representation in the US-Iran deal, according to Iran International.

headlines.title: IRGC moves to seize historic Protestant church in Tehran - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: The IRGC is reportedly moving to seize a historic Protestant church in Tehran, as reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: Ghalibaf details $12bn Iranian assets with unrestricted central bank access - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: Ghalibaf detailed $12 billion in Iranian assets with unrestricted central bank access, according to Iran International.

headlines.title: Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: An 'Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities' has been published, as reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: Khamenei to end Eje'i’s judiciary tenure after one term
headlines.snippet: Khamenei is reportedly set to end Eje'i’s tenure in the judiciary after one term, according to Iran International.

headlines.title: Industrial sector grows 4.8% in Q1 2026, drives 40% of economic growth — JCI
headlines.snippet: The industrial sector grew by 4.8% in Q1 2026, contributing 40% of economic growth, according to the Jordan Times.

headlines.title: Israeli settlement expansion ‘tool of colonial cleansing, elimination’ — Royal committee
headlines.snippet: A royal committee described Israeli settlement expansion as a "tool of colonial cleansing, elimination," as reported by the Jordan Times.

headlines.title: Syria president names last members to finalise first post-Assad parliament
headlines.snippet: Syria's president has named the final members of parliament, finalizing the first parliament since the Assad era, according to the Jordan Times.

headlines.title: US, Iran officials hold indirect talks in Doha
headlines.snippet: US and Iranian officials have held indirect talks in Doha, as reported by the Jordan Times.

headlines.title: World Bank approves $700 million in financing to boost investment, jobs in Jordan
headlines.snippet: The World Bank has approved $700 million in financing to stimulate investment and jobs in Jordan, according to the Jordan Times.

headlines.title: Lebanon announces in Paris its intention to abolish the death penalty
headlines.snippet: Lebanon has announced its intention to abolish the death penalty during an event in Paris, as reported by L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: Trump says US, Iran 'getting along very well'; Aoun insists framework agreement with Israel 'safeguards Lebanon’s rights both legally and operationally'
headlines.snippet: Trump stated that the US and Iran are "getting along very well," while Aoun insisted the framework agreement with Israel safeguards Lebanon’s rights, according to L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: US signs agreement to build permanent embassy in Jerusalem
headlines.snippet: The United States has signed an agreement to construct a permanent embassy in Jerusalem, as reported by L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: House of Representatives rejects resolution calling for 'withdrawal of US armed forces from all hostilities in Lebanon'
headlines.snippet: The House of Representatives rejected a resolution that called for the withdrawal of US armed forces from all hostilities in Lebanon, according to L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: Indirect Iran-US technical talks underway in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: Indirect technical talks between Iran and the US are currently underway in Doha, according to a source cited by L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: White House lifts export control on Anthropic that froze its most advanced models
headlines.snippet: The US government has lifted export controls on Anthropic’s most advanced AI models, including Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. The company plans to restore access soon.

headlines.title: Oman has delivered a proposal on the future of the Strait of Hormuz to the US, sources say
headlines.snippet: Oman has presented a proposal to the US and allies regarding the future of the Strait of Hormuz. The unfinalized plan outlines a system for shipping companies to pay service fees.

headlines.title: The Strait of Hormuz pain has been China’s gain, The Asia Group says
headlines.snippet: China has become the primary beneficiary of the Strait of Hormuz closure, according to The Asia Group. This has unevenly impacted Asian economies reliant on imports.

headlines.title: US, Iranian officials hold indirect, lower-level talks in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: US and Iranian officials are engaged in indirect, lower-level technical talks in Doha, facilitated by Qatari and Pakistani mediators. US special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met Qatar’s prime minister.

headlines.title: Egypt’s Former Irrigation Minister warns not to underestimate impact of GERD
headlines.snippet: Egypt's former Minister of Water Resources and Irrigation, Mohamed Nasr-Eddin Allam, warned against underestimating the repercussions of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) on Egypt and Sudan.

headlines.snippet: Lebanon's government disaster risk management unit issued its weekly report, noting 420 operational shelters with 46,341 displaced individuals, a decrease since the ceasefire.

headlines.snippet: Vice President Al Sheikh affirmed the mobilization of all available resources to mitigate the impacts of the economic crisis, according to WAFA Agency.

headlines.snippet: The Knesset has passed the preliminary reading of a bill to limit mosque loudspeakers, as reported by WAFA Agency.

headlines.snippet: Israeli forces reportedly assaulted mourners at a funeral in Jerusalem, according to WAFA Agency.

headlines.snippet: Colonists blocked roads and sprayed racist graffiti in Wadi Sa’ir, Hebron, according to WAFA Agency.

headlines.snippet: The Foreign Ministry rejected statements by the Board of Peace that diminish UNRWA's role, according to WAFA Agency.

headlines.snippet: Two martyrs and several wounded resulted from an "Israeli" shelling northwest of Gaza, indicating ongoing violations, according to paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: The occupation authorities have issued demolition notices for three residences in Hebron, according to paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: Gold has fallen to its lowest level in seven months, while oil prices have risen, according to paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: Seven people died when a tourist boat capsized at Saifullah Lake in northwestern Pakistan, as reported by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: Uganda has detected an isolated case of the Marburg virus, according to the Africa CDC and Arab News.

headlines.snippet: A fire in a Belgian apartment block resulted in multiple fatalities, as reported by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: Sri Lanka has ended its state of emergency, which had been in place since a deadly cyclone in 2025, according to Arab News.

headlines.snippet: Spain recorded over 1,000 heat-related deaths in June, as reported by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: A masked couple was arrested after scaling the Empire State Building in New York and unfurling a peace banner. The incident followed a marriage proposal.

headlines.snippet: Tehran has vowed an "immediate powerful response" to any threats against the Iranian people and leadership. Iran's foreign minister stated that Trump has committed the US to restraining its allies in Tel Aviv.

headlines.snippet: The French parliament has backed a military spending plan of nearly $500 billion. This plan aims to incorporate lessons from recent conflicts and enhance capabilities in areas like drones and missiles.
